skill.name: pydanticai-docs
skill.description: Use this skill to answer Pydantic AI framework questions about agents, tools, dependencies, structured outputs, models, testing, and installation.
skill.summary: Reference guidance for building and testing Pydantic AI agents.
skill.author: DougTrajano

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many external command and credential patterns, but review shows they are Markdown inline names and Python examples in SKILL.md, not executable skill logic. The only confirmed risk factor is an explicit instruction to fetch official Pydantic AI documentation from a hardcoded URL, which is legitimate but requires network access. No prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Fetch
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to fetch the complete Pydantic AI documentation from https://ai.pydantic.dev/llms.txt. This is a clear network dependency, but the destination is the official documentation site and no credential or user data transfer is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network instruction is explicit and tied to a fixed documentation URL. Risk is low because there is no evidence of exfiltration, dynamic URL construction, or hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections correspond to Markdown inline identifiers, Python code examples, and shell installation examples. SKILL.md contains no runnable script file, command dispatcher, or instruction to execute untrusted user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are contained in Markdown prose or fenced examples. No executable wrapper, hook, or automation path is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Credential Pattern Is Illustrative Placeholder
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes an api_key field inside a dependency dataclass example. This documents dependency injection and does not access environment variables, transmit secrets, or request real credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The api_key text appears in a small example class and is never read from an environment variable or sent over a network. The static env_access finding is therefore a false positive.

content.value_statement: Pydantic AI projects often need precise patterns for agents, tools, dependencies, and structured outputs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused reference guidance for building typed Python agent workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Pydantic AI agents, tools, toolsets, dependencies, and model identifiers.
Provides quick reference examples for synchronous, asynchronous, and streaming agent runs.
Shows how to use Pydantic models for structured outputs.
Describes dependency injection with RunContext and typed dependency objects.
Covers testing with TestModel and agent override patterns.
Points assistants to the full official Pydantic AI documentation URL for deeper reference.
limitations: It is a documentation reference, not an executable code generator.
It may need live documentation access for complete or current API details.
It does not validate installed package versions in a local project.
Examples are concise and may require adaptation for production systems.

use_cases.title: Create a Typed Agent
use_cases.description: Use the skill to design a Pydantic AI agent with dependencies, tools, and a structured output model.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check whether an agent uses deps_type, output_type, retries, and test overrides correctly.

use_cases.title: Learn Pydantic AI Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the skill to understand core framework concepts through short examples and terminology.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pydanticai-docs skill to explain Pydantic AI agents, tools, dependencies, and structured outputs in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an Agent Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pydanticai-docs skill to design a Pydantic AI agent for my use case. Include the deps_type, output_type, tools, and run style.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Agent Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pydanticai-docs skill to review my Pydantic AI agent implementation. Focus on type safety, dependency injection, retries, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pydanticai-docs skill to propose a production Pydantic AI pattern with typed outputs, toolsets, streaming, tests, and error handling.

output_examples.input: How should I add structured output to a Pydantic AI agent?
output_examples.output: The response explains defining a Pydantic model, passing it as the agent output type, and reading the validated result object.

output_examples.input: Review my agent that passes user data into tools.
output_examples.output: The response identifies whether dependencies are typed, whether tools receive RunContext, and whether sensitive values are handled deliberately.

output_examples.input: What is the difference between a tool and a toolset?
output_examples.output: The response compares individual agent tools with grouped FunctionToolset usage and explains when each pattern fits.

best_practices: Provide your Pydantic AI version or allow documentation lookup for version-sensitive questions.
Ask for typed dependencies and output models when designing agent workflows.
Use test models or overrides when requesting testing guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to execute package installation commands without reviewing them first.
Do not paste real API keys when discussing dependency examples.
Do not assume the short examples cover every production concern.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps answer Pydantic AI questions about agents, tools, dependencies, structured outputs, streaming, installation, and testing.

faq.question: Does it run Pydantic AI code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill that guides an assistant in explaining or drafting Pydantic AI patterns.

faq.question: Why does it mention fetching documentation?
faq.answer: It points to the official Pydantic AI documentation file so the assistant can check complete framework details when needed.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it require secrets?
faq.answer: No. One example names an api_key field, but users should never paste real secrets into prompts.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production guidance?
faq.answer: It can support production planning, but you should verify current Pydantic AI documentation and test the final implementation.
